Table of Contents

DrawnUI for .NET

Source Code 👈

A rendering engine for .NET, including MAUI, Blazor, and platform-agnostic hosts, built on top of SkiaSharp

Hardware-accelerated rendering engine for .NET, with packages and hosts for MAUI, Blazor, and DrawnUi.Net, powered by SkiaSharp.

📦 Choose Your Package

Platform and package guide:

  • Platforms and Packages

For .NET MAUI:

dotnet add package DrawnUi.Maui

For Blazor WebAssembly:

dotnet add package DrawnUi.Blazor.Wasm

For Blazor Server:

dotnet add package DrawnUi.Blazor.Server

For platform-agnostic .NET workflows:

dotnet add package DrawnUi.Net

Initialize in MauiProgram.cs when using MAUI:

builder.UseDrawnUi();

👉 Start with Platforms and Packages, then jump into the runtime-specific guide.

📚 Knowledge Base

Documentation & Guides

  • Platforms and Packages - Choose the right package and host before you start
  • .NET MAUI - Native-host setup, MAUI guidance, and MAUI tutorials
  • Installation and Setup - .NET MAUI installation and setup
  • MAUI Tutorials - MAUI-only tutorials and example projects
  • Porting Native to Drawn - MAUI-focused migration guidance from native controls to DrawnUI
  • Blazor - Entry point for DrawnUI in Blazor
  • Blazor Packages - Package roles, install targets, and project reference layout
  • Blazor WebAssembly - Browser-side Canvas setup and local rendering guidance
  • Blazor Server - server-backed Canvas setup, server-rendering model, and current limits
  • Blazor Hybrid Web App - Mixed InteractiveServer and InteractiveWebAssembly app structure
  • Blazor Capabilities - Runtime fit, validated strengths, and current boundaries
  • Blazor Migration - Adoption strategy for existing Blazor apps
  • Blazor FAQ - Package choice, migration path, and support boundaries
  • DrawnUi.Net - Platform-agnostic rendering, harnesses, and headless workflows
  • Fluent Extensions - Code-behind UI creation patterns
  • FAQ - Frequently asked questions and answers
  • Controls Documentation - Complete controls reference
  • Advanced Features - Performance and platform topics

Community & Support

  • GitHub Discussions - Community help and discussions
  • GitHub Issues - Report bugs or ask questions

Additional Resources

  • Sample Apps - Apps built with DrawnUI
  • How DrawnUI was created - article by the creator

Can't find what you're looking for? → Ask in GitHub Discussions - The community is here to help!

Features

🎨 Rendering & Graphics

  • Hardware-accelerated SkiaSharp rendering with max performance
  • Pixel-perfect controls with complete visual customization
  • 2D and 3D transforms for advanced visual effects
  • Visual effects for every control: filters, shaders, shadows, blur
  • Caching system for optimized re-drawing performance

😍 Development Experience

  • Design in XAML or code-behind - choose your preferred approach
  • Fluent C# syntax for programmatic UI creation
  • Hot Reload compatible for rapid development iteration
  • Virtual controls - no native views/handlers, background thread accessible

🚀 Performance & Optimization

  • Optimized rendering - only visible elements drawn
  • Template recycling for efficient memory usage
  • Hardware acceleration on all supported platforms
  • Smooth animations targeting maximum FPS

👆 Interaction & Input

  • Advanced gesture support - panning, scrolling, zooming, custom gestures
  • Keyboard support - track any key combination
  • Touch and mouse input handling
  • Multi-platform input normalization

🧭 Navigation & Layout

  • Familiar MAUI Shell navigation techniques on canvas
  • SkiaShell + SkiaViewSwitcher for fully drawn app navigation
  • Modals, popups, toasts and custom overlays
  • Enhanced layout system with advanced positioning
Edit this page
Made by Nick Kovalsky aka AppoMobi (@taublast)
